text:
BACKGROUND
Wearable inertial measurement units can quantify movement outside a motion laboratory. Reliable interpretation depends on sensor placement and calibration.

METHODS
The rehabilitation device sampled tri-axial acceleration and angular velocity at 100 Hz. Each sensor was aligned to an anatomical segment before the exercise session. A synchronization pulse aligned the wearable stream with the reference system.

RESULTS
The calibrated signals preserved the timing of repeated knee flexion cycles. Drift increased when the alignment step was omitted.

queries.query: At what rate did the rehabilitation device sample motion?
queries.relevant_passage: The rehabilitation device sampled tri-axial acceleration and angular velocity at 100 Hz.

queries.query: How was the wearable stream synchronized with the reference system?
queries.relevant_passage: A synchronization pulse aligned the wearable stream with the reference system.

text:
1. SENSOR PREPARATION
Force-sensitive resistors were conditioned with ten loading cycles before calibration. The sensing surface was mounted beneath the insole without folding the active area.

2. CALIBRATION
Known loads from 10 N to 200 N were applied in ascending and descending order. Resistance was recorded after a two-second settling interval at each load. A logarithmic model was fitted separately for each sensor.

3. VALIDATION
Validation loads were not used during model fitting. Median absolute error was reported across all held-out loads.

queries.query: What loads were applied during force sensor calibration?
queries.relevant_passage: Known loads from 10 N to 200 N were applied in ascending and descending order.

queries.query: Which loads were excluded from model fitting?
queries.relevant_passage: Validation loads were not used during model fitting.

text:
SCOPE
This guidance describes evaluation of an assistive clinical prediction model. It does not replace clinical judgment or local governance.

DATA QUALITY
Missing measurements were quantified for every deployment site. Subgroup performance was reported when sample size permitted stable estimates.

MONITORING
The deployed service logged model version, input schema version, and prediction latency. A predefined alert triggered review when calibration drift exceeded the accepted range.

LIMITATIONS
Performance on retrospective data does not establish prospective clinical benefit.

queries.query: What condition triggered review of the deployed service?
queries.relevant_passage: A predefined alert triggered review when calibration drift exceeded the accepted range.

queries.query: Why is retrospective performance not enough to prove clinical benefit?
queries.relevant_passage: Performance on retrospective data does not establish prospective clinical benefit.
